finding_results.notes: Reworked all six anchored beats so the distinction lands through exposed-wound imagery, the sneaker detail, stripped delivery, jealous specificity, direct term-setting, and concrete repayment consequence instead of repeated contrastive-negation scaffolding.
finding_results.summary: The chapter no longer leans on the same 'it was not/this was not' sentence engine at the cited pressure points.

finding_results.notes: Shifted the emotional pressure out of the room-as-barometer pattern and into Lena's breath and teeth, June's household objects, chair-and-knee compression, the price of the sneakers on the table, and the sink/fridge/homework details that keep the betrayal embedded in ordinary life.
finding_results.summary: The revised scene now varies its pressure cues through body, object, and domestic geography rather than repeatedly making the room itself do the emotional work.

summary: Addressed both prose-copyedit findings by replacing repeated contrastive-negation rhythms with sharper concrete detail, relocating key pressure beats from abstract room-proxy phrasing into Lena's body and charged household objects, and preserving the chapter's raw confrontation, continuity, and character register.
